name: Braised Whole Fillet of Salmon (Julia Child)
recipeIngredient: 1 Large carrot and onion cut into neat 1/4-inch dice
2 Or 3 tender celery stalks, neatly diced
2 lb Skinless fillet of salmon - about 1/2 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
MASTER RECIPE Braised Whole Fillet of Salmon in Wine and Aromatic Vegetables
For 6 to 8 servings:
THE AROMATIC VEGETABLES:
1. Cook the diced vegetables slowly in the butter until quite tender but not browned - about 10 minutes.
2. Season lightly with salt, pepper, and a big pinch of dried tarragon.
PREPARING THE FISH:
3. Go over the salmon carefully with your fingers to detect any little bones; pull them out with tweezers or pliers.
4. Score the skin side of the fish.
5. Dust with salt and pepper, and place the best side up in the baking dish.
ASSEMBLING:
6. Spread the cooked diced vegetables over the fish.
7. Pour 1/2 inch of vermouth around it.
8. Cover the fish with the wax paper, buttered side down.
BAKING: 12 to 15 minutes at 350F
9. Preheat the oven to 350F.
10. Set the fish in the lower middle level.
11. When beginning to bubble lightly, baste the surface with the liquid in the dish, basting several times again until the flesh feels lightly springy to the touch.
VARIATIONS:
12. AU NATURAL: Braised Salmon Served in Its Own Juices
13. Quickly boil down the cooking juices in the saucepan until almost syrupy.
14. Pour them over the fish and vegetables, and serve.
15. AROMATIC WHITE BUTTER SAUCE
16. The usual and lovely butter sauce of modern cookery can be as rich and buttery as you wish - from 3 or 4 tablespoons to half a pound.
17. Using the preceding boiled-down juices as a base, proceed to beat in the butter as in the lemon-butter sauce for the broiled fish on page 83.
18. WINEY CREAM SAUCE
19. A reasonable and equally delectable compromise is a light veloute sauce made with the cooking juices, then boiled down with cream:
20. Cook together 2 1/2 tablespoons butter and 3 tablespoons flour for 2 minutes without coloring.
21. Off heat, whisk in the hot braising juices and 1 cup heavy cream.
22. Boil slowly until reduced to 1 1/2 cups; season carefully.
(Full details for veloute sauce are on page 272.)
23. Either serve the fish cloaked in its vegetables and accompany with the sauce, or fold the vegetables into the sauce and spoon over the fish.
